title: Crusader Atlas — Sites
description: GeoJSON FeatureCollection of Crusader-era sites (castles, churches, fortified towns, battles, sieges) across the Latin East, 1099-1291. Each Feature is a Point with rich historical metadata.

metadata.description: Export metadata block common to all Crusader Atlas data files.

pointGeometry.description: GeoJSON Point geometry. Coordinates are [longitude, latitude] in WGS84 decimal degrees.

id.description: URL-safe kebab-case slug, matching the landing-page path and deep-link query parameter. Derived from canonical_name via slugify().

internal_key.description: Original internal key from the source DATA[] array (_key field). Preserves the raw canonical_name casing for backward compatibility with the build pipeline.

name.description: Display name of the site (canonical_name from the source gazetteer, with DISPLAY_NAMES overrides applied).

alternate_names.description: Other names by which the site is known (Arabic, Hebrew, Frankish, modern).

territory.description: Modern country or administrative region (e.g., 'Israel', 'Lebanon', 'Jordan', 'Syria', 'Egypt', 'West Bank', 'Gaza Strip', 'Golan Heights').

modern_location.description: Modern city and country string (e.g., 'Acre, Israel').

lordship.description: Medieval lordship this site fell within, computed via point-in-polygon against the lordship polygons. Null for sites outside the Kingdom of Jerusalem (e.g., Egypt, inland Syria). Cross-references lordships.geojson by name.

coordinate_precision.description: 'exact' when coordinates are surveyed or GPS-verified; 'approximate' when placed from textual or cartographic evidence.

description.description: Curated prose summary of the site's history, construction, and significance. Author-verified; never AI-generated.

fortification.description: Fortification assessment. Null for battle and siege entries, where the concept does not apply.

has_extended_narrative.description: Present only on battle/siege sites. True if battles.json contains an extended narrative for this site.

image.description: Photo filename (bare, no path prefix) referencing a file in fortress-pics/, or a remote https:// URL. Null if no image is available.

wikipedia.description: Wikipedia article links for this site.

en.description: URL of the English Wikipedia article, or null.

he.description: URL of the Hebrew Wikipedia article, or null.

status.description: Confidence level that this site was fortified during the Crusader period.

visible_remains.description: Whether Crusader-period structural remains are visible at the site today.

harbor.description: Whether the site had or was associated with a medieval harbor.
